column1: Security Breach in Medical Data,"Noted unauthorized access incidents on medical data within the hospital's system. This may have occurred due to phishing attacks or outdated security protocols. So far, enhanced firewall settings have been implemented, and staff awareness training has been conducted to prevent future incidents. We kindly request your assistance in investigating the matter and providing recommendations for improved security measures.",0,0

Problems with Timeline Synchronization,"Hello Customer Support, I am experiencing difficulties with my project timelines not syncing across different devices. I have tried restarting, reviewing my settings, and updating my software, but the issue remains unresolved. It seems there might be a problem with the integration or compatibility. I would greatly appreciate any help in addressing this issue. It is causing significant delays and reducing my productivity. I look forward to your response. Kind regards, [Your Name]",0,0

Despite restarting the servers and reviewing the logs, the issue has not been resolved. I would be grateful if you could address this urgently and offer a solution promptly.",0,0

Translation Cooperation Invitation,"Hi client, Hope you are doing well. This is Lydia from Top Translation Company,I wanted to see if it’d make sense for us to connect and discuss capabilities around providing Translation and Localization in 300+ languages & 30+ industries. Our services include: 1. Translation (Certificate /Handbook / Book, etc.) 2. Localization (Website / Software / Games, etc.) 3. Multimedia ( Dubbing / Transcription /Subtitling, etc.) 5. Interpretation（Simultaneous interpretation / Consecutive interpretion,etc.) As a gesture of building trust, we are pleased to offer a free translation of up to 300 words for your project. If you have any questions or require further information, please feel free to let me know. 、 If you are not interested in our services at this time, kindly reply with ""No."" Best regards Lydia Tan Lydia Tan| Global Business Developer Mobile: +86 17711789917 Skype：Lydia Tan5632 Respond with Unsub if you don't want to receive any further communication. Capabilities of API Integration in Project Management,"Customer Support, seeking details on API integration capabilities for project management in a SaaS environment. Could you provide information on the available APIs, their functionalities, and documentation resources to assist us in getting started? We are particularly interested in integrating this platform with our existing tools and workflows. Specifically, we would like to know about the types of data that can be accessed and manipulated through the API, as well as any limitations or restrictions. Additionally, it would be helpful to know about the support resources available for developers and integrators.",0,0

Customer Support Ticket,"Dear Customer Support, I am in need of your assistance as I have encountered a data breach through an insecure network. The root cause is due to insufficient encryption and out-of-date firmware. Despite my efforts to address the issue by restarting the router and reviewing the firewall settings, the problem continues to persist. I would greatly appreciate your help in securing my network and preventing future breaches. Could you kindly offer guidance on how to update the firmware and enhance encryption? Additionally, I would be grateful for any supplementary advice to strengthen the security of my network. Thank you for your attention and assistance. I eagerly await your response. Sincerely, [Your Name]",0,0
Suggestion for IAM service - Group-based permissions,"Hi Aetheros Support Team,

I'm writing to suggest a potential improvement for your IAM service. It would be incredibly useful if we could manage permissions based on groups, rather than assigning them individually to each user.
This would significantly streamline our user management process, especially as our team grows.
Thank you for considering this suggestion.

Accepting new submissions for upcoming issues - Journal of Anaesthesia,"Dear Josh, Greetings from Mia James!!! I hope this message finds you well. On behalf of the editorial team at Journal of Anaesthesia, I would like to extend an invitation to submit your latest research for publication in our esteemed journal. The journal is international in scope and comprehensive in coverage. We publish original articles on all aspects of general and regional anaesthesia, intensive care and pain therapy, including research on equipment. We are pleased to notify you that each article in the Journal of Anaesthesia has a unique DOI (Digital Object Identifier), ensuring your research is easy to find, reference, and cite across various platforms. The DOI ensures that your work is permanently accessible, increasing its visibility and impact in the academic community. To submit your manuscript attach a file as an attachment to our editor email id : miajames@directivepublctions.org or submit via our online submission portal: Click here to Submit your manuscript. If you have any questions or need further information, feel free to contact us. We look forward to the possibility of featuring your research. Best regards, Mia James Journal of Anaesthesia Email: miajames@directvepub.com",1,0

Viktig info om oppdateringen av visningsnavn på FINN,"Hei! Tidligere denne uken mottok du en e-post fra oss i FINN der vi informerte om at vi har endret visningsnavnet på din FINN-profil. Det gikk dessverre litt fort i svingene da vi gjorde denne endringen, og vi har derfor endret visningsnavnet ditt tilbake til slik det var før. Som vi nevnte i vår første epost, ble endringene av visningsnavn gjort som et ekstra sikkerhetstiltak. Den siste tiden har vi sett en del tilfeller der folk med uærlige hensikter har prøvd å gjette de private e-postadressene til FINN-brukere, basert på visningsnavnet deres på FINN. Etter å ha gjennomført endringen, ser vi imidlertid at det ikke var noen større sammenheng mellom visningsnavnet ditt og mailadressen vi har registrert på deg, og derfor har vi endret visningsnavnet ditt tilbake - beklager bryderiet! Vi oppfordrer fortsatt alle våre brukere til å ha et bevisst forhold til visningsnavnet sitt, og være klar over at dette kan brukes for å prøve å lure deg hvis det er veldig likt din virkelige identitet/navn. Dersom du ser at visningsnavnet ditt på FINN enkelt kan kobles til en av dine e-postadresser, oppfordrer vi deg til å endre visningsnavnet. Det kan du enkelt gjøre på Min FINN - Min konto - trykk på blyanten ved “Om deg”. Beklager om vi har skapt litt forvirring med dette, vi ønsker å hjelpe våre brukere til å ha det trygt når de bruker FINN. Ta gjerne kontakt med oss hvis du har spørsmål – vi er her for å hjelpe! Hilsen oss i FINN",0,0

column2: use 5.10.0 warns,"On 10/3/07, Robin Barker wrote: > > > > > This could be implemented as bit 0x01000000 in $^H, or as $^H{v_string_ok}. > > > > > > A clearer solution (but more obtrusive) would be to introduce a separate > > > warning category (portable_vstring) and C (and above) would do > > > an implicit C, just as C does > > > an implicit C. > > > Well, technically those solutions are equivalent, but the %^H one avoids using > > a further bit in the warning bit vector. > > I tried to implement the %^H approach, but I could work out how to set/get %^H > in C code in way that actually achieved the right effect. If you look in regcomp in pregcomp (i think) you can find code the gets from %^H. Setting from C I havent done. Yves -- perl -Mre=debug -e ""/just|another|perl|hacker/""",0,0
